summary: Kenneth demonstrates executing the covered call wheel strategy on HIM and NPhase stocks to generate weekly premium income. He shows multiple entry points, strike selection logic, and premium collection of $540 in one week across three positions, explaining assignment scenarios and profit outcomes.
takeaways: Sell covered calls 1 week out when entering position late in trading week to optimize premium collection
Strike selection: sell calls at or very close to purchase price ($50 strike on $50 HIM purchase, $41.50 strike on $41.50 NPhase purchase) to maximize premium while accepting assignment
Premium collection prioritized over capital gains: $24-$193 premium per call is the primary profit target, not stock appreciation
Assignment outcome math: retain all premiums regardless of assignment; if assigned, realize small profit ($33-$34) plus premium; if not assigned, keep shares plus premium ($370+ total)
Buy dips opportunistically within same strategy: re-buy HIM at $50 after assignment, buy NPhase again at $38.66 after initial drop for another call sale
Target weekly premium income of $540 minimum across portfolio ($180/week stated goal appears conservative relative to actual results)
Multiple simultaneous positions on different stocks compress timeline and reduce single-stock risk while compounding premium income

strategy_candidates.change: Establish minimum premium threshold per position: only sell calls generating $20+ premium on sub-$50 stocks, $30+ on $50+ stocks to ensure time-value adequacy for weekly rotation
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth shows $24 premium on HIM ($50) and $144-$193 on NPhase ($38-$41) positions; these thresholds prevent low-premium assignments that don't justify capital deployment

strategy_candidates.change: Maintain 3-4 simultaneous covered call positions across different stocks rather than single-stock focus to achieve $500+/week target with lower individual stock concentration risk
strategy_candidates.evidence: Three simultaneous positions (HIM x2, NPhase x2) generated $540/week; demonstrates portfolio compounding vs. single-stock limitation

title: Part 5, 6/30 - can I make $180 Per Week?
